data:
{"id":1,"type":"non-technical","question":"Walk me through the engineering design process","answer":"A) Begin by setting the scope of the problem
     - What are the inputs and outputs
     - Where does this "thing" interface with other systems?
     - What are the requirements (operational environment, expected use case, etc)

A2) Trade studies
     - Look at different approaches to the problem
     - see what is available and done already

B) Consider manufacturing processes
     - Material choice
     - Conventional subtractive machining?
     - 3d printing?
          - SLA
          - DMLS
     - Sourcing off the shelf?

B2) Simulations
     - If necessary go through any simulations to prove a design may be viable in concept

C) Prototyping
      - Goals?
          - Conceptual, proof of concept, characterization?
     - Main goal is to remove as many unknown factors before you commit to a more expensive manufacturing method or components

Testing to prove calculations/simulations vs qualification testing!

D) Testing
     - List the things the object in question will be exposed to, and create a test environment or tests that will be able to verify it will work in those expected environments
     - Ideally iterate as much as possible in prototyping phase, to reduce cost and turn around times. But it is unavoidable that iteration sometimes happens with production hardware.","created_at":"2026-03-01 00:23:33","updated_at":"2026-03-04 22:06:35","user_id":"3e00a304-193a-4b95-8589-5c57c16ef21d"}
